sections.label: Section 890
sections.original: τὰς ἀμφί σοι κλαίουσα λαμπτηρουχίας
sections.translation: with weeping for the beacon-lights set for you but always neglected . The faint whir of the buzzing gnat often waked me from dreams in which I beheld more disasters to you than the time of sleep could have compassed .

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: νῦν ταῦτα πάντα τλᾶσ ἀπενθήτῳ φρενὶ
sections.translation: But now , having born all this , my heart freed from its anxiety , I would hail my husband here as the watchdog of the fold , the savior forestay of the ship , firm-based pillar of the lofty roof , only-begotten son of a father , or land glimpsed by men at sea beyond their hope ,

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: κάλλιστον ἦμαρ εἰσιδεῖν ἐκ χείματος ,
sections.translation: dawn most fair to look upon after storm , the gushing stream to thirsty wayfarer — sweet is it to escape all stress of need . Such truly are the greetings of which I deem him worthy . But let envy [*] be far removed , since many were the ills

sections.label: Section 905
sections.original: ἠνειχόμεσθα . νῦν δέ μοι , φίλον κάρα ,
sections.translation:
we endured before .
And now , I pray you , my dear lord , dismount from your car , but do not set on common earth the foot , my King , that has trampled upon Ilium . To her attendants Why this loitering , women , to whom I have assigned the task to strew with tapestries the place where he shall go ?

sections.label: Section 910
sections.original: εὐθὺς γενέσθω πορφυρόστρωτος πόρος
sections.translation: Quick ! With purple let his path be strewn , that Justice may usher him into a home he never hoped to see . The rest my unslumbering vigilance shall order duly , if it please god , even as is ordained .

sections.label: Section 914
sections.original: Λήδας γένεθλον , δωμάτων ἐμῶν φύλαξ ,
sections.translation: Offspring of Leda , guardian of my house ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: ἀπουσίᾳ μὲν εἶπας εἰκότως ἐμῇ :
sections.translation: your speech fits well with my absence ; for you have drawn it out to ample length . But becoming praise — this prize should rightly proceed from other lips . For the rest , pamper me not as if I were a woman , nor , like some barbarian , [*]

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: χαμαιπετὲς βόαμα προσχάνῃς ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: grovel before me with widemouthed acclaim ; and do not draw down envy upon my path by strewing it with tapestries . It is the gods we must honor thus ; but it is not possible for a mortal to tread upon embroidered fineries without fear .

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: λέγω κατ ἄνδρα , μὴ θεόν , σέβειν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: I tell you to revere me not as a god , but as a man . Footmats and embroideries sound diverse in the voice of Rumor ; to think no folly is the best gift of the gods . Only when man ’ s life comes to its end in prosperity dare we pronounce him happy ;

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: εἰ πάντα δ ὣς πράσσοιμ [*] ἄν , εὐθαρσὴς ἐγώ .
sections.translation: and if I may act in all things as I do now , I have good confidence .

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: καὶ μὴν τόδ εἰπὲ μὴ παρὰ γνώμην ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Come now , tell me this , in accordance with your mind .

sections.label: Section 932
sections.original: γνώμην μὲν ἴσθι μὴ διαφθεροῦντ ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Purpose ! Be assured that I shall not corrupt my mind .

sections.label: Section 933
sections.original: ηὔξω θεοῖς δείσας ἂν ὧδ ἔρδειν τάδε ;
sections.translation: You would in fear have vowed to the gods to act thus .

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: εἴπερ τις , εἰδώς γ εὖ τόδ ἐξεῖπον τέλος .
sections.translation: If someone with full knowledge had pronounced this word .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: τί δ ἂν δοκεῖ [*] σοι Πρίαμος , εἰ τάδ ἤνυσεν ;
sections.translation: What do you suppose that Priam would have done , if he had achieved your triumph ?

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: ἐν ποικίλοις ἂν κάρτα μοι βῆναι δοκεῖ . [*]
sections.translation: He would have set foot upon the embroideries , I certainly believe .

sections.label: Section 937
sections.original: μή νυν τὸν ἀνθρώπειον αἰδεσθῇς [*] ψόγον .
sections.translation: Then do not be be ashamed of mortal reproach .

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: φήμη γε μέντοι δημόθρους μέγα σθένει .
sections.translation: And yet a people ’ s voice is a mighty power .

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: ὁ δ ἀφθόνητός γ οὐκ ἐπίζηλος πέλει .
sections.translation: True , yet he who is unenvied is unenviable .

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: οὔτοι γυναικός ἐστιν ἱμείρειν μάχης .
sections.translation: Surely it is not woman ’ s part to long for fighting .

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: τοῖς δ ὀλβίοις γε καὶ τὸ νικᾶσθαι πρέπει .
sections.translation: True , but it is right for the happy victor to yield the victory .

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: ἦ καὶ σὺ νίκην τήνδε δήριος τίεις ;
sections.translation: What ? is this the kind of victory in strife that you prize ?

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: πιθοῦ : κράτος μέντοι πάρες γ [*] ἑκὼν ἐμοί .
sections.translation: Oh yield ! Yet of your own free will entrust the victory to me .

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: ἀλλ εἰ δοκεῖ σοι ταῦθ , ὑπαί τις ἀρβύλας
sections.translation: Well , if you will have your way ,

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: λύοι τάχος , πρόδουλον ἔμβασιν ποδός .
sections.translation: quick , let some one loose my sandals , which , slavelike , serve the treading of my foot ! As I walk upon these purple vestments may I not be struck from afar by any glance of the gods ’ jealous eye . A terrible shame it is for one ’ s foot to mar the resources of the house by wasting wealth and costly woven work .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: τούτων [*] μὲν οὕτω : τὴν ξένην δὲ πρευμενῶς
sections.translation: So much for this . This foreign girl receive into the house with kindness . A god from afar looks graciously upon a gentle master ; for no one freely takes the yoke of slavery . But she ,

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ἄνθος , στρατοῦ δώρημ , ἐμοὶ ξυνέσπετο .
sections.translation:
the choicest flower of rich treasure , has followed in my train , my army ’ s gift .
Since I have been forced to obey you and must listen to you in this , I will tread upon a purple pathway as I pass to my palace halls .

sections.label: Section 958
sections.original: ἔστιν θάλασσα , τίς δέ νιν κατασβέσει ;
sections.translation: There is the sea ( and who shall drain it dry ?) producing stain of abundant purple , costly as silver

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: κηκῖδα παγκαίνιστον , εἱμάτων βαφάς .
sections.translation: and ever fresh , with which to dye our clothes ; and of these our house , through the gods , has ample store ; it knows no poverty . Vestments enough I would have devoted to be trampled underfoot had it been so ordered in the seat of oracles

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: ψυχῆς κόμιστρα τῆσδε μηχανωμένῃ . [*]
sections.translation: when I was devising a ransom for your life . For if the root still lives , leaves come again to the house and spread their over-reaching shade against the scorching dog star ; so , now that you have come to hearth and home , you show that warmth has come in wintertime ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: ὅταν δὲ τεύχῃ Ζεὺς ἀπ [*] ὄμφακος πικρᾶς
sections.translation: and again , when Zeus makes wine from the bitter grape , [*] then immediately there is coolness in the house when its rightful lord occupies his halls . As Agamemnon enters the palace O Zeus , Zeus , you who bring things to fulfilment , fulfill my prayers ! May you see to that which you mean to fulfill ! Exit

sections.label: Section 1.975
sections.original: τίπτε μοι τόδ ἐμπέδως
sections.translation: Why does this terror so persistently hover standing before my prophetic soul ? Why does my song , unbidden and unfed , chant strains of augury ? Why does assuring confidence not sit on my heart ’ s throne

sections.label: Section 1.980
sections.original: οὐδ ἀποπτύσαι [*] δίκαν
sections.translation: and spurn the terror like an uninterpretable dream ? But Time has collected the sands of the shore upon the cables cast thereon

sections.label: Section 1.985
sections.original: ψαμμίας ἀκάτας [*] παρή-
sections.translation: when the shipborn army sped forth for Ilium . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.988
sections.original: πεύθομαι δ ἀπ ὀμμάτων
sections.translation: Of their coming home I learn with my own eyes and need no other witness .

sections.label: Section 1.990
sections.original: τὸν δ ἄνευ λύρας ὅμως [*] ὑμνῳδεῖ
sections.translation: Yet still my soul within me , self-inspired , intones the lyreless dirge of the avenging spirit , and cannot wholly win its customary confidence of hope .

sections.label: Section 1.995
sections.original: σπλάγχνα δ οὔτι [*] ματᾴζει
sections.translation: Not for nothing is my bosom disquieted as my heart throbs against my justly fearful breast in eddying tides that warn of some event . But I pray that my expectation may fall out false

sections.label: Section 1.1000
sections.original: ἐς τὸ μὴ τελεσφόρον .
sections.translation: and not come to fulfilment .

sections.label: Section 2.1001
sections.original: μάλα γέ τοι [*] τὸ μεγάλας ὑγιείας
sections.translation: Truly blooming health does not rest content within its due bounds ; for disease ever presses close against it , its neighbor with a common wall . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.1005
sections.original: καὶ πότμος εὐθυπορῶν
sections.translation: So human fortune , when holding onward in straight course strikes upon a hidden reef . And yet , if with a well-measured throw , caution heaves overboard

sections.label: Section 2.1010
sections.original: σφενδόνας ἀπ εὐμέτρου ,
sections.translation: a portion of the gathered wealth , the whole house , with woe overladen , does not founder nor engulf the hull . [*] Truly the generous gift from Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 2.1015
sections.original: φιλαφής τε καὶ ἐξ ἀλόκων ἐπετειᾶν
sections.translation: rich and derived from yearly furrows , makes an end of the plague of famine .

sections.label: Section 2.1017
sections.original: τὸ δ ἐπὶ γᾶν πεσὸν [*] ἅπαξ θανάσιμον
sections.translation: But a man ’ s blood , once it has first fallen by murder to earth

sections.label: Section 2.1020
sections.original: προπάροιθ [*] ἀνδρὸς μέλαν αἷμα τίς ἂν
sections.translation: in a dark tide — who by magic spell shall call it back ? Even he [*] who possessed the skill to raise from the dead — did not Zeus make an end of him as warning ?

sections.label: Section 2.1025
sections.original: εἰ δὲ μὴ τεταγμένα
sections.translation: And unless one fate ordained of the gods restrains another fate from winning the advantage , my heart would outstrip my tongue and pour forth its fears [*] ;

sections.label: Section 2.1030
sections.original: νῦν δ ὑπὸ σκότῳ βρέμει
sections.translation: but , as it is , it mutters only in the dark , distressed and hopeless ever to unravel anything in time when my soul ’ s aflame .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: εἴσω κομίζου καὶ σύ , Κασάνδραν λέγω ,
sections.translation: Get inside , you too , Cassandra [*] ; since not unkindly has Zeus appointed you to share the holy water of a house where you may take your stand , with many another slave , at the altar of the god who guards its wealth . Get down from the car and do not be too proud ;

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: καὶ παῖδα γάρ τοι φασὶν Ἀλκμήνης ποτὲ
sections.translation: for even Alcmene ’ s son [*] , men say , once endured to be sold and eat the bread of slavery . But if such fortune should of necessity fall to the lot of any , there is good cause for thankfulness in having masters of ancient wealth ; for they who , beyond their hope , have reaped a rich harvest of possessions ,

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: ὠμοί τε δούλοις πάντα καὶ παρὰ στάθμην .
sections.translation: are cruel to their slaves in every way , even exceeding due measure . You have from us such usage as custom warrants .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: σοί τοι λέγουσα παύεται σαφῆ λόγον .
sections.translation: It is to you she has been speaking and clearly . Since you are in the toils of destiny , perhaps you will obey , if you are so inclined ; but perhaps you will not .

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ἀλλ εἴπερ ἐστὶ μὴ χελιδόνος δίκην
sections.translation: Well , if her language is not strange and foreign , even as a swallow ’ s , I must speak within her comprehension and move her to comply .

sections.label: Section 1053
sections.original: ἕπου . τὰ λῷστα τῶν παρεστώτων λέγει .
sections.translation: Go with her . With things as they now stand , she gives you the best . Do as she bids and leave your seat in the car .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: οὔτοι θυραίᾳ [*] τῇδ [*] ἐμοὶ σχολὴ πάρα
sections.translation: I have no time to waste with this woman here outside ; for already the victims stand by the central hearth awaiting the sacrifice — a joy we never expected to be ours . As for you , if you will take any part , make no delay .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: εἰ δ ἀξυνήμων οὖσα μὴ δέχει λόγον ,
sections.translation: But if , failing to understand , you do not catch my meaning , then , instead of speech , make a sign with your barbarian hand .

sections.label: Section 1062
sections.original: ἑρμηνέως ἔοικεν ἡ ξένη τοροῦ
sections.translation: It is an interpreter and a plain one that the stranger seems to need . She bears herself like a wild creature newly captured .

sections.label: Section 1064
sections.original: ἦ μαίνεταί γε καὶ κακῶν κλύει φρενῶν ,
sections.translation: No , she is mad and listens to her wild mood ,

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ἥτις λιποῦσα μὲν πόλιν νεαίρετον
sections.translation: since she has come here from a newly captured city , and does not know how to tolerate the bit until she has foamed away her fretfulness in blood . No ! I will waste no more words upon her to be insulted thus . Exit

sections.label: Section 1069
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ , ἐποικτίρω γάρ , οὐ θυμώσομαι .
sections.translation: But I will not be angry , since I pity her .

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ἴθ , ὦ τάλαινα , τόνδ ἐρημώσασ ὄχον ,
sections.translation: Come , unhappy one , leave the car ; yield to necessity and take upon you this novel yoke .

sections.label: Section 1.1072
sections.original: ὀτοτοτοτοῖ πόποι δᾶ .
sections.translation: Woe , woe , woe ! O Apollo , O Apollo !
